memory_candidates.content: When adding new task-creating commands to the agent system, the load_when configuration in index.json for state-management-schema.md and state-management.md must be updated to include the new command. Otherwise the command will create TODO.md entries without access to the authoritative format spec, leading to inconsistent entries.

memory_candidates.content: When rewriting documentation files that exist in both deployed (.claude/context/) and extension source (.claude/extensions/core/context/) locations, always sync both copies and verify with diff. The extension source is the canonical location that gets merged during loader operations, while the deployed copy is what agents actually read. Also check and update line_count in index.json entries.

memory_candidates.content: The generate_claudemd() function in shared/extensions/merge.lua builds .claude/CLAUDE.md by concatenating a header template from extensions/core/templates/claudemd-header.md with content fragments from each loaded extension's merge-sources/claudemd.md or EXTENSION.md. The sync operation (sync.lua) uses a different code path (inject_section with section markers). Both paths must be kept consistent when modifying generation behavior.

memory_candidates.content: The extension system's CLAUDE.md is generated via merge.lua:generate_claudemd() as a fully computed artifact -- NOT via section injection with markers. The inject_section()/remove_section() functions still exist in merge.lua but are unused for CLAUDE.md. The section_id field in manifest merge_targets.claudemd is vestigial. Both load and unload call generate_claudemd() after state update.

The extension loader supports 12 provides categories: agents, skills, commands, rules, context, scripts, hooks, data, docs, templates, systemd, root_files. The loader.lua file has 12 public functions corresponding to these. Unload dependency handling is a hard block (not a warning with confirm) -- the function returns false before reaching the confirmation dialog.

active_projects.description: Update or consolidate the 3 stale sister files in .claude/context/meta/: architecture-principles.md (references .opencode, phantom 3-level context model, non-existent status-sync-manager/git-workflow-manager), standards-checklist.md (references subagent-return.md, non-existent frontmatter fields temperature/max_tokens/can_delegate_to, non-existent tools), interview-patterns.md (references old interview stages). These were identified as follow-on work during task 485 research. Each file should be rewritten to match the current system or removed if redundant with the new meta-guide.md. Both deployed (.claude/context/meta/) and extension source (.claude/extensions/core/context/meta/) copies must be synced. Update index.json line counts if file sizes change.

memory_candidates.content: All agents in .claude/agents/ use minimal frontmatter (name, description, optional model). The extensive frontmatter schema documented in standards-checklist.md (temperature, max_tokens, can_delegate_to, version, mode, agent_type, permissions, context_loading, delegation, lifecycle) was never implemented. The actual standard is documented in agent-frontmatter-standard.md.

The phantom components status-sync-manager and git-workflow-manager appear in 42 files across .claude/ context. The actual components are skill-status-sync and skill-git-workflow. This is a widespread naming inconsistency that predates the current skill-based architecture.

When deleting context files from .claude/context/meta/, there are typically 4 places in extensions.json that reference them: installed_files, data_skeleton_files, merged_sections.index.paths, plus the main index.json and extension index-entries.json. All must be updated atomically. The extensions.json uses an object keyed by extension name (not an array), so access via .extensions.core rather than .extensions[].

active_projects.description: Fix /meta prompt mode regression: model bypasses Skill delegation and implements changes directly instead of creating tasks via interactive picker

artifacts.summary: Meta bypass analysis: 4 enforcement gaps identified with concrete fixes
Implementation plan with 4 phases across 3 waves
Implementation summary: 4-phase anti-bypass enforcement for /meta command

memory_candidates.content: The /meta command bypass regression is caused by a missing Anti-Bypass Constraint section (present in /plan, /research, /implement but absent from /meta) combined with no PostToolUse hook monitoring writes to .claude/ paths. The validate-plan-write.sh hook only covers specs/*/plans/*.md, specs/*/reports/*.md, and specs/*/summaries/*.md. Fix requires: (1) Anti-Bypass section in meta.md, (2) new hook for .claude/ writes, (3) skill-level reinforcement.

The three-layer anti-bypass pattern for command enforcement consists of: (1) Anti-Bypass Constraint section in the command .md file with mechanism-focused prohibition language, (2) PostToolUse hook with advisory additionalContext (not blocking) that detects path patterns, (3) reinforcement in both skill and agent definitions. The hook must exclude legitimate paths (e.g., specs/) and be advisory to avoid interfering with /implement which legitimately writes to the same paths.

active_projects.completion_summary: Added three-layer anti-bypass enforcement to /meta command: Anti-Bypass section in meta.md, PostToolUse hook validate-meta-write.sh, and SCOPE BOUNDARY in meta-builder-agent. All synced to extension sources.
active_projects.claudemd_suggestions: Added Anti-Bypass Constraint to commands/meta.md, created hooks/validate-meta-write.sh, added SCOPE BOUNDARY to agents/meta-builder-agent.md, added Anti-Bypass to skills/skill-meta/SKILL.md, registered hook in settings.json
